title: File Servers, Object Storage, CDNs, and Architecture Selection · Execution Trace: Filesystem Object Cdn
summary: A product sends every byte through one web process, stores uploads on an ephemeral disk, and adds a CDN without deciding authority, mutation, privacy, or failure boundaries. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the filesystem object cdn increment in architecture and responsibility decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evide…
objectives: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
Plan deterministic browser models, Go compile and race gates, disposable integration, controlled AWS tests, load, fault, restore, security, cost, and production evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Filesystem Object Cdn · Theory Filesystem Object Cdn
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 5852 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to select a file, object, gateway, direct, or CDN path from trust and volume evidence; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare local filesystems, shared filesystems, object storage, databases, origin services, and CDNs by interface, consistency, scale, trust, and operations.
